vulnerability.title: Modify the System Login Banner
vulnerability.description: To configure the system login banner edit /etc/issue. Replace the default text with a message compliant with the local site policy or a legal disclaimer. The DoD required text is either: You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only. By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions: -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations. -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS. -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose. -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests -- not for your personal benefit or privacy. -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details. OR: I've read & consent to terms in IS user agreem't.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then login_banner_text='' # Multiple regexes transform the banner regex into a usable banner # 0 - Remove anchors around the banner text login_banner_text=$(echo "$login_banner_text" | sed 's/^\^\(.*\)\$$/\1/g') # 1 - Keep only the first banners if there are multiple # (dod_banners contains the long and short banner) login_banner_text=$(echo "$login_banner_text" | sed 's/^(\(.*\.\)|.*)$/\1/g') # 2 - Add spaces ' '. (Transforms regex for "space or newline" into a " ") login_banner_text=$(echo "$login_banner_text" | sed 's/\[\\s\\n\]+/ /g') # 3 - Adds newlines. (Transforms "(?:\[\\n\]+|(?:\\n)+)" into "\n") login_banner_text=$(echo "$login_banner_text" | sed 's/(?:\[\\n\]+|(?:\\\\n)+)/\n/g') # 4 - Remove any

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing /etc/issue to insert the required banner text. This is a straightforward file modification that does not affect authentication mechanisms, networking, or critical services, and does not require a reboot.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to remediate banner_etc_issue by editing /etc/issue to contain required text. We must follow workflow: PLAN, then REVIEW via review_plan, then APPLY.

First we need to propose plan: read /etc/issue to see current content, then write new content that replaces entire file with required banner. The required banner text is the DoD text provided. Probably we need to insert that entire message. Provide plan description. Then call review_plan.

We need to propose a plan: backup /etc/issue, write full DoD banner text (need exact text). We must include full text as per rule description, seems long. Provide the text fully.

We need to first read the current /etc/issue to backup. Then write backup. Then write new content. Ensure permissions.

Plan steps:
1. read_file /etc/issue
2. write_file backup /etc/issue.bak with same content, mode 0644
3. write_file new content to /etc/issue (full banner), mode 0644
4. run_cmd chmod 0644 /etc/issue; chown root:root /etc/issue
5. verify by reading file and checking that it contains expected lines (maybe grep). Use run_cmd grep -q some snippet.

We must submit plan to review_plan first.

review_feedback: The remediation creates a backup and restores correct ownership/permissions, but it writes only a truncated version of the required DoD banner. The OpenSCAP rule expects the full disclaimer with all clauses and specific line breaks, so the vulnerability will remain.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan updates /etc/issue but the proposed banner text is truncated and does not include the full DoD disclaimer required by the rule. As written, the remediation is unlikely to pass the OpenSCAP check.
concerns: Banner text is incomplete; missing required clauses and exact wording.
Formatting (line breaks) may be required by the rule but plan writes a single line.
No backup of the original /etc/issue is created before overwriting.
No verification step to confirm the banner now matches the expected pattern.
suggested_improvements: Insert the complete DoD banner text exactly as described, preserving required line breaks.
Create a backup of the current /etc/issue (e.g., /etc/issue.bak) before modification.
After writing, verify the content with grep or a checksum against the expected pattern.
Ensure file ownership is root:root and permissions are 0644.
Consider using a configuration management tool or script that is idempotent.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation creates a backup and restores correct ownership/permissions, but it writes only a truncated version of the required DoD banner. The OpenSCAP rule expects the full disclaimer with all clauses and specific line breaks, so the vulnerability will remain.
concerns: Banner text is incomplete; several required clauses are missing.
Line breaks and formatting do not match the specification required by the rule.
Verification step only checks the first line, not the complete content of /etc/issue.
The plan may pass basic file ownership checks but will fail the content compliance check.
suggested_improvements: Insert the full DoD disclaimer exactly as defined in the rule, preserving all required line breaks and punctuation.
Use a heredoc (cat <<'EOF' > /etc/issue ...) to write the multi‑line banner safely.
After writing, verify the entire file content (e.g., compare a checksum or grep for multiple unique strings) to ensure full compliance.
Make the script idempotent by checking if the current banner already matches the desired content before overwriting.
